pillars.name: Resolution Rate
pillars.dataSource: Per-message thumbs up/down reviews (column Z in chat messages)
pillars.measures: Did the chatbot solve the user's problem on a per-message basis?
pillars.weightRationale: Highest weight (40%) — most direct per-message signal with the largest sample (41,908 reviews), making it the primary quality indicator for chatbot effectiveness

pillars.name: Sentiment Health
pillars.dataSource: NLP sentiment classification on every message (SENTIMENT column)
pillars.measures: How frustrated are customers during conversations?
pillars.weightRationale: Reduced to 15% — broadest signal (covers all messages via NLP) but less precise than direct feedback and partially correlated with Resolution Rate, avoiding double-counting

pillars.name: Session Satisfaction
pillars.measures: Overall session experience as rated by the customer
pillars.weightRationale: 20% weight reflecting high signal quality — explicit end-of-session star ratings represent genuine customer intent, despite very low capture volume (0.48% of sessions)

pillars.name: Escalation & Failure
pillars.dataSource: SENT_TO_SKY column + feedback categories (Needed human support, Technical issue)
pillars.measures: When does the chatbot completely fail and need human intervention?
pillars.weightRationale: Elevated to 25% — escalation to a human agent is a critical failure event with direct cost and UX impact; low current rate (2.5%) makes monitoring its increase a business priority
